rows.Notas: Annual global event on smart cities and urban innovation. Held at Fira de Barcelona Gran Via, this event brings together city leaders, policymakers and companies to share solutions across governance, mobility, energy and sustainability
Annual deep learning symposium held in Barcelona. Focus on research presentations from local and international groups. The 2025 edition takes place at Cosmocaixa Barcelona.
Evento de dos dias en Casa Llotja de Mar. Reune a founders, inversores y actores del ecosistema tech con keynotes, paneles y networking
Feria global de tecnologia y telecom, estamos en 4YFN. Revisar opciones de stand y agenda de reuniones. Apertura de registro varios meses antes.

Annual technology conference in Lisbon drawing startups, investors, corporates and policymakers. It covers topics across technology, innovation and venture. Held at MEO Arena and Lisbon Exhibition & Congress Centre.
Annual European high-performance computing summit, gathering HPC providers, users and policy makers across Europe.
Barcelona Supercomputing Center hackathon around MareNostrum resources. Update dates when official confirmation is published.
Global HPC event with exhibition and technical program for high performance computing and related fields. Official SC26 dates should be published mid-2025.

Supports deep tech research at early TRL (1–4). Eligible are research teams or consortia with legal entities in EU Member States or Horizon Europe Associated Countries. Offers grants up to ~€4M per project.

Eligible: research organisations, universities, SMEs, startups, consortia
Region: EU + Horizon Europe Associated Countries
Amount: up to ~€4M (varies by sub-call)

Aims to help projects that have proven results move towards commercial validation and business planning. Projects must be based on results from EIC Pathfinder, ERC Proof of Concept or other eligible Horizon Europe projects.

Eligible: start-ups, SMEs, research organisations and small consortia
Region: EU + Associated Countries
Amount: up to ~€2.5M

Spanish national programme for technological innovation programmes led by technology centres. Must be a grouping of Technology Centres (4–7 entities) coordinated by a centre.

Eligible: technology centres grouped in consortia
Region: Spain
Amount: typical eligible project size ~€2M–€6M

Supports start-ups and SMEs to develop and scale deep tech innovations. Combines grants (up to ~€2.5M) and equity investments (typically €0.5M–€10M). Higher equity funding possible via STEP Scale Up.

Eligible: SMEs and start-ups established in EU or associated countries
Region: EU + Associated Countries
Amount: grant up to ~€2.5M plus possible equity investment

Eligible region: Regional
Amount: €20.000
Requirements: The research team must be responsible for the work and hold the rights to the results used in the project.

Eligible region: Regional
Amount: €30.000
Requirements: The project must have a defined product or service scope.
